title: Crusader Atlas — Lordships
description: GeoJSON FeatureCollection of lordships, fiefs, and sea zones of the Crusader states. Each Feature is a Polygon with administrative metadata, heraldry, and lists of historical lords.

metadata.description: Export metadata block common to all Crusader Atlas data files.

polygonGeometry.description: GeoJSON Polygon geometry. Coordinates are arrays of linear rings; each ring is an array of [lon, lat] positions. The first and last position of each ring must be identical (closed ring).

items.description: A linear ring of [lon, lat] positions. Must have at least 4 positions (3 vertices + closing duplicate).

coordinates.description: Array of linear rings. The first is the exterior ring; subsequent rings are holes.

id.description: Kebab-case slug derived from the lordship name. Cross-referenced by the lordship field in sites.geojson.

name.description: Display name of the lordship, fief, or sea zone.

capital.description: Name of the capital city or stronghold of this lordship, if applicable.

parent.description: Name of the parent lordship or kingdom this territory belongs to.

style.description: Visual styling hints for rendering the polygon on the map.

fill.description: CSS color value for polygon fill (e.g. '#b3cde3').

coat_of_arms.description: Filename of the coat of arms image in coat-of-arms/, or null if none exists.

photo.description: Filename of a representative photo in fortress-pics/, or a remote URL. Null if none available.

description.description: Prose paragraphs describing the lordship's history and significance. Each array element is one paragraph.

lords.description: Chronological list of lords, rulers, or holders of this territory.

lord_column_header.description: Custom column header for the lords table (e.g. 'King', 'Count', 'Lord'). Null uses a default.

note.description: Editorial note or caveat about this lordship entry.

lord.description: A single lord or ruler entry in the chronological succession list.

name.description: Name of the lord or ruler. Null for gaps or unknown holders.

reign.description: Reign or tenure period as a display string (e.g. '1110-1131').

wikipedia.description: URL of the English Wikipedia article for this lord, or null.
